Label: Generalized Institutional Distrust
Description: This class is characterized by a high probability of selecting Category 1 across all institutions, most notably for political parties (0.86) and the police (0.75).

Label: High Institutional Trust
Description: This class is characterized by very high probabilities of selecting Category 7 across most national institutions, most notably for the president (0.84), public ministry (0.77), and armed forces (0.75). Trust is slightly more distributed for political parties and the media, though Category 7 remains the most probable response for all items.

Label: Moderate Institutional Trust
Description: This class predominantly selects mid-to-high categories (4 and 5) across most institutions, with the highest trust levels concentrated in the armed forces, the president, and the electoral tribunal.

Label: Selective Executive and Military Trust
Description: This class shows high trust in the president (P=0.45 for Category 7) and armed forces (P=0.36 for Category 7), while expressing strong distrust of political parties (P=0.26 for Category 1) and the police (P=0.20 for Category 2).

Label: Low-Trust Institutional Skeptics
Description: This class is characterized by high probabilities of low trust in political parties and the police, while maintaining a moderate, diffuse level of trust across other national institutions and a relatively higher trust in the president.

Label: Strong Institutional Trust
Description: This class is characterized by a strong concentration of responses in Categories 5 and 6 across all institutions, with particularly high trust in the president, armed forces, and supreme court.
